jokes.text: They consider me a competitor because of my natural stealth and ambivalence.
jokes.setup: Abed says cats don't like Annie
jokes.punchline: Annie explains with completely absurd self-assessment of having cat-like qualities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie character beat - her tendency to over-analyze and create elaborate explanations for simple things. Great word choice with 'ambivalence' in this context.

jokes.text: Oh, did she get high again and forget she doesn't wear contacts?
jokes.setup: Someone's vision appears impaired
jokes.punchline: Abed's matter-of-fact reference to Britta's drug use affecting her perception
jokes.explanation: Good character work for Abed's deadpan delivery style and knowledge of his friends' quirks.

jokes.text: Well, he's really got his teeth in my flesh right now. Okay, he's touching bone. I'm just waiting for him to finish up. Not a lot I can do at this point.
jokes.setup: Annie confidently says cats love her and goes to pet one
jokes.punchline: Calm, matter-of-fact description of being violently mauled by the cat
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and commitment to the bit. Annie's polite, resigned acceptance of being mauled is perfectly in character and creates great physical comedy through description.

jokes.text: Britta's a rich genius with super powers and she's gonna live on our sofa for no reason.
jokes.setup: Abed calls his previous exposition embarrassing
jokes.punchline: Delivers even more obvious exposition while claiming to fix it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta-commentary. The joke works on multiple levels - mocking exposition while being exposition, and Abed's awareness of TV tropes.

jokes.text: Oh, no. Dinosaurs. My tax documents. Facts about Native Americans. Just kidding.
jokes.setup: Dean appears to be dropping important documents
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's carrying absurd random items, then 'just kidding'
jokes.explanation: Typical Dean randomness but the misdirection structure works. The 'just kidding' deflates it slightly.

jokes.text: Did Greendale need a virtual reality system? Like a hole in the head.
jokes.setup: Dean announces VR purchase for the school
jokes.punchline: Frankie's dry assessment using common idiom
jokes.explanation: Standard straight-man response. Works for character but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: A hole in the head is something that you don't need. She said through a huge hole in her head.
jokes.setup: Frankie uses idiom 'like a hole in the head'
jokes.punchline: Dean explains the idiom then points out her literal mouth as a 'hole in her head'
jokes.explanation: Dean's tendency to over-explain and find literal interpretations. Mildly clever but not exceptional.

jokes.text: The last people I saw using it were in the Honeycomb Hideout.
jokes.setup: Discussion about VR being outdated
jokes.punchline: Reference to cereal commercial as last place VR was seen
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that feels organic to the conversation about outdated tech.

jokes.text: You don't know for sure that I'm not a veteran, and nobody can say for sure that today isn't Veteran's Day.
jokes.setup: Jeff needs an excuse to avoid helping Britta move
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously vague excuse that technically can't be disproven
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff Winger lawyer logic applied to avoiding responsibility. The absurd specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Relax, my little bean countess.
jokes.setup: Frankie expresses concern about spending
jokes.punchline: Dean's flirty nickname playing on 'bean counter' accounting term
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that fits Dean's flirtatious personality and tendency toward elaborate nicknames.

jokes.text: Have fun in the Stone Age while I step into the third dean-mension.
jokes.setup: Dean about to enter VR while others use regular computer
jokes.punchline: Pun combining 'third dimension' with his name
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean pun. Not groundbreaking but perfectly in character and moderately clever.

jokes.text: And Jesus wept, for there were no more worlds to conquer.
jokes.setup: Dean accomplishes basic task of making font bigger
jokes.punchline: Dramatically quotes Alexander the Great about conquering worlds for a mundane achievement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of grandiose historical quote with mundane computer task. The absurd overreaction is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: I feel like everybody's talking about that haircut I gave Vicki, so-.
jokes.setup: Britta talking about her new job prospects
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of apparently giving someone a terrible haircut
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Britta's obliviousness to her own incompetence. Creates comedy through implication.

jokes.text: I wanted a Murphy bed for shenanigans but the cost to shenanigan ratio was too high.
jokes.setup: Explaining furniture choices for Britta
jokes.punchline: Treating 'shenanigans' as a quantifiable economic unit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed line - applying analytical thinking to absurd concepts. 'Cost to shenanigan ratio' is brilliantly constructed phrase.

jokes.text: It's Portugal's answer to Gremlins if Gremlins' question was how can you totally rip us off without spending any money and having watched us maybe twice?
jokes.setup: Describing Portuguese Gremlins movie
jokes.punchline: Complex joke structure about low-budget international knockoffs
jokes.explanation: Sophisticated joke structure that perfectly captures Abed's film knowledge and analytical style. The meta-construction is brilliant.

jokes.text: Is settings the volcano or the cobbler's workshop? It's a monastery.
jokes.setup: Dean trying to navigate VR interface
jokes.punchline: VR system has absurdly themed interface elements for basic functions
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about overly complicated VR interface design. Sets up the running gag about the system's pretentiousness.

jokes.text: You can do that by dragging it to the accessories and peripherals castle, and planting it in the printer driver.
jokes.setup: Dean needs to print a file
jokes.punchline: Absurdly elaborate process described matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the VR absurdity. The contrast between Frankie's deadpan delivery and the ridiculous instructions works well.

jokes.text: You're not the new Annie. You're the new Abed. I don't know what that means yet.
jokes.setup: Jeff comparing Frankie to other group members
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on character types within the show structure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on Community's self-awareness about character archetypes and cast changes.

jokes.text: The green ones are bad and the red ones are kind of like isolationists. It's a metaphor for World War II.
jokes.setup: Explaining Portuguese Gremlins plot
jokes.punchline: Finding deep political meaning in trashy knockoff movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed - finding intellectual depth in B-movies. The casual delivery of the WWII analysis makes it funnier.

jokes.text: That's what people say when they take your soul, and they rip it out of your chest and then shove it in your mouth.
jokes.setup: Being told she's being dramatic
jokes.punchline: Extremely dramatic metaphor proving the accusation right
jokes.explanation: Great self-defeating argument where Britta proves she IS being dramatic

jokes.text: I got bit by a cat.
jokes.setup: Chang appears randomly in emotional scene
jokes.punchline: Still focused on his minor injury while others deal with bigger problems
jokes.explanation: Good running gag and perfectly timed interruption. Chang's self-obsession works for the character.

jokes.text: I remember the New Year's glasses having zeros for eyes. And when did Stella get her groove back?
jokes.setup: Jeff claiming he's bad with dates
jokes.punchline: Mixing specific pop culture memory with vague movie reference
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Jeff's selective memory and vanity

jokes.text: Poor. We're poor friends.
jokes.setup: Britta asking what kind of friends they are
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation focusing on their financial status
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and misdirection - perfect Jeff deflection

jokes.text: I eat love, Britta. Hot, delicious love that you were willing to wipe your ass with.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining why he enjoyed Britta's parents' cooking despite his diet
jokes.punchline: Romantic metaphor that crashes into crude reality
jokes.explanation: Great Jeff Winger line - starts poetic and crashes into harsh reality. The structure mirrors his character arc.

jokes.text: When my phone rings and I say hello, and they say, is Pablo there? I don't say, who's asking, I say, wrong number.
jokes.setup: Elroy explaining social convention about asking for names
jokes.punchline: Overthinking simple phone etiquette with elaborate example
jokes.explanation: Good character introduction for Elroy - shows his tendency toward unnecessary elaboration and logical analysis.

jokes.text: That doesn't mean anything. I can copy a file by holding my finger down on a phone. Well, so can a monkey.
jokes.setup: Jeff points out VR system is unnecessarily complicated vs phone simplicity
jokes.punchline: Elroy dismisses phone interface as primitive
jokes.explanation: Good back-and-forth establishing Elroy's character as someone who overcomplicates things while dismissing simple solutions.

jokes.text: You bet your sweet ass I saw Lawnmower Man. I consulted on it. Took Brigitte Nielsen to the premiere.
jokes.setup: Jeff mentions terrible 90s VR movie Lawnmower Man
jokes.punchline: Elroy not only saw it but was professionally involved and had glamorous date
jokes.explanation: Great character development - shows Elroy's past glory and current fall. The Brigitte Nielsen detail adds perfect 90s specificity.

jokes.text: And my feet aren't meaty!
jokes.setup: Jeff storming out after confrontation
jokes.punchline: Defensive about random insult from earlier in scene
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff vanity moment - storming out dramatically but can't let the physical insult slide.

jokes.text: We sent you a birthday card to your apartment in New York, and then next week you're setting fire to a Jamba Juice in San Jose.
jokes.setup: Parents explaining difficulty staying in touch with Britta
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Britta committing arson
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and perfect Britta character work. The casual delivery of 'setting fire to a Jamba Juice' is comedy gold.

jokes.text: Your mom and I think you'd be the prettiest. Absolutely, the boys would be swiping right. Or girls! Yes! Whatever makes you happy. Totally.
jokes.setup: Parents trying to be supportive about Britta's dating life
jokes.punchline: Awkward over-enthusiasm and trying too hard to be progressive
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing parents trying too hard to be cool and supportive. The escalating enthusiasm makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Who are these doddering open-minded geezers? Where are the people who wouldn't let me dress up like Prince for Halloween?
jokes.setup: Britta confused by her parents' current supportive attitude
jokes.punchline: Wants her restrictive parents back, misses being oppressed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta character moment - she's built her identity on rebelling, so supportive parents threaten her sense of self.

jokes.text: Or had me drug tested when I was 11 because they said I was laughing too much?
jokes.setup: Listing childhood grievances against parents
jokes.punchline: Absurd paranoid parenting taken to extreme
jokes.explanation: Dark and absurd - drug testing an 11-year-old for happiness is perfectly wrong

jokes.text: She's growing up.
jokes.setup: Britta storming off dramatically on a children's bicycle
jokes.punchline: Parent's understated response to daughter's tantrum
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement with visual comedy of adult on child's bike

jokes.text: I put a tilde on this n! I can do anything.
jokes.setup: Dean has been in VR for hours accomplishing basic tasks
jokes.punchline: Treating adding accent mark as ultimate achievement
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dean's delusions of grandeur. The specific mention of 'tilde' makes it funnier than generic computer task.

jokes.text: To use a phrase I coined in the 90s, I'm going in. You coined that? Oh yeah, lock and load. That's mine too.
jokes.setup: Elroy about to enter VR to help Dean
jokes.punchline: Claims to have invented common action movie phrases
jokes.explanation: Perfect character work for Elroy - delusional about his past influence while casually claiming credit for pop culture staples.

jokes.text: You know, one of the most unfair lessons we're forced to learn is that our parents are human beings. We wanna think of them as gods or demons, because then that would make us heroes.
jokes.setup: Jeff counseling Britta about her parent issues
jokes.punchline: Insightful observation about why we mythologize parents - to cast ourselves as heroes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant writing - genuine wisdom delivered through comedy. Shows Jeff's growth while being universally relatable insight.

jokes.text: Here's your power. Yeah. Now, manipulate worlds.
jokes.setup: Dean wants power to manipulate worlds in VR
jokes.punchline: Elroy hands him a regular computer mouse
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of the VR pretension. The visual gag of the mundane mouse being 'power to manipulate worlds' is brilliant.

jokes.text: All that matters is that we're all going to die.
jokes.setup: Britta trying to make peace with parents and explain life lessons
jokes.punchline: Arrives at morbid conclusion as wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta - trying to be profound but landing on dark pessimism. Character-consistent attempt at wisdom.

jokes.text: Now, it's just gonna be margaritas, Pottery Barn, fighting our family's tendency toward obesity as we slide into oblivion. First you guys, and then me quickly thereafter.
jokes.setup: Britta describing her future relationship with parents
jokes.punchline: Morbid escalation from middle-age activities to death timeline
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor escalation from domestic activities to mortality - very Britta

jokes.text: Benjamin. I got bit by a cat, Deb.
jokes.setup: Tender family reunion moment
jokes.punchline: Chang interrupts with his ongoing medical complaint, treats stranger like close friend
jokes.explanation: Good callback and perfectly timed interruption. Chang's inappropriate familiarity with strangers is in character.

jokes.text: And that, my friend, is the Greendale effect. I'll never get out of here, will I? I haven't met many that do.
jokes.setup: Elroy considering enrolling at Greendale
jokes.punchline: Dean acknowledges school as inescapable trap
jokes.explanation: Great meta-commentary on the show's premise and character arcs. Acknowledges the absurdity of everyone staying at community college forever.

jokes.text: There are times when change needs to be different, then there's changes that are more the same. But the same changes don't tend to be different than the changes that came before.
jokes.setup: Abed providing episode-ending philosophical commentary
jokes.punchline: Completely circular, meaningless pseudo-wisdom about change
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta-commentary that sounds profound but is actually nonsensical. Parodies TV show ending monologues while being one.
